title: How to Set Up Stripe Payments
summary: Step by step guide to connecting Stripe so guests can pay online by card at checkout.
content:
Stripe is the online card payment system used by GAS. Once connected, guests can pay by credit or debit card directly on your booking checkout page.

Before you start, you will need a Stripe account. If you do not have one, go to stripe.com and register for free.

STEP 1 — Get your Stripe API keys
1. Log in to your Stripe account at dashboard.stripe.com
2. Click 'Developers' in the top menu
3. Click 'API keys'
4. You will see two keys:
   - Publishable key — starts with pk_live_ (or pk_test_ for testing)
   - Secret key — starts with sk_live_ (or sk_test_ for testing)
5. Copy both keys — you will need them in GAS

Note: Use pk_test_ and sk_test_ keys first to test payments without real charges. Switch to pk_live_ and sk_live_ when you are ready to accept real bookings.

STEP 2 — Add your keys to GAS
1. In GAS Admin, go to Bookings & Revenue
2. Click Payments
3. Find the Stripe section
4. Paste your Publishable key into the Publishable Key field
5. Paste your Secret key into the Secret Key field
6. Click Save

STEP 3 — Test your setup
1. Make a test booking on your site using a test card number
2. Stripe test card: 4242 4242 4242 4242, any future expiry date, any 3-digit CVC
3. Check your Stripe dashboard to confirm the test payment appeared

If you see an error, double check that you have copied the full key without any spaces.

STEP 4 — Go live
When you are ready to accept real payments, replace the test keys with your live keys (pk_live_ and sk_live_) and save again.

Need help? Contact the GAS team and we will walk you through it.

title: How to Connect Your Custom Domain
summary: How to point your own domain name (e.g. myhotel.com) to your GAS website so it goes live on your own address.
content:
By default your GAS website runs on a GAS address (e.g. yoursite.sites.gas.travel). This guide explains how to connect your own domain name so guests see your own web address.

Before you start, you will need:
- Access to your domain registrar (e.g. GoDaddy, Namecheap, OVH, 1&1, IONOS)
- Your custom domain name (e.g. myhotel.com)

STEP 1 — Add your domain in GAS
1. In GAS Admin, go to Create Websites
2. Find your site and click Settings or Custom Domain
3. Enter your domain name (e.g. myhotel.com or www.myhotel.com)
4. Click Save — GAS will show you the IP address or CNAME value you need
5. Note down the value shown — you will need it in the next step

STEP 2 — Update your DNS settings
Log in to your domain registrar (where you bought your domain) and find the DNS settings.

To point your root domain (myhotel.com):
- Add an A Record
- Host / Name: @ (or leave blank)
- Value / Points to: the IP address shown in GAS
- TTL: 3600 (or Auto)

To point www (www.myhotel.com):
- Add a CNAME Record
- Host / Name: www
- Value / Points to: the CNAME value shown in GAS
- TTL: 3600 (or Auto)

STEP 3 — Wait for DNS to update
DNS changes can take between 15 minutes and 48 hours to fully update across the internet. Usually it is much faster — often under an hour.

You can check if your domain has updated by visiting your domain in a browser. When it shows your GAS website, it is live.

STEP 4 — SSL Certificate
GAS automatically generates a free SSL certificate (https://) for your domain once DNS is connected. This usually happens within a few minutes of the DNS update completing.

Common problems:
- Site not showing after 48 hours: double check the A Record or CNAME value was entered correctly with no extra spaces
- SSL not working: wait a little longer — it can take up to 30 minutes after DNS updates
- Not sure which registrar you use: check your domain purchase email or search whois.net for your domain

Need help? Contact the GAS team with your domain name and we will check the setup for you.
